title: Life Biosciences Closes $80M Series D to Advance Cellular Reprogramming Trial
description: Life Biosciences, the David Sinclair co-founded biotech, closed a fully-subscribed $80M Series D to fund its landmark Phase 1 trial of ER-100, the first human test of partial epigenetic reprogramming. Capital extends runway into H2 2027 and validates cellular rejuvenation as an investable longevity category.

title: Sex Differences in Immune Aging Reshape Inflammaging Models
description: A Barcelona Supercomputing Center single-cell RNA study of ~1,000 people published this week shows women develop sharper age-related inflammatory immune shifts while men accumulate pre-leukemia cells. The finding suggests sex-specific geroprotective protocols may be needed and has immediate implications for inflammaging biomarker design.

title: Rapamycin May Blunt Exercise Response in Humans - Trial Implications
description: Fresh Lifespan.io coverage of human data indicates rapamycin may interfere with exercise adaptations, a result that complicates long-running dosing debates. The signal lands as UT Health San Antonio launches an 84-person NIA-funded rapamycin RCT and the Coit-funded 700-person trial led by Dr. Bonnie LaFleur moves forward.

title: USPTO Publishes Major Senolytic Compound Patent Covering 800K+ Molecules
description: USPTO published application US20260097022A1 disclosing ML-driven senolytic discovery across 2,352 screened compounds and neural-net predictions across 800,000+ molecules targeting Bcl-2. The filing marks the largest public senolytics IP disclosure to date and signals the pipeline is broadening well beyond dasatinib-plus-quercetin.

title: WHOOP $575M Series G at $10.1B Valuation Marks Consumer Healthspan Inflection
description: WHOOP closed a $575M round at a $10.1B valuation - nearly triple its prior mark - with Mayo Clinic, Abbott, Qatar Investment Authority and Mubadala on the cap table. The raise crystallizes consumer healthspan as an institutional category and sets up a likely IPO path, with revenue run rate reaching $1.1B.

title: Life Biosciences Secures $80 Million Series D Financing
description: David Sinclair co-founded Life Bio closed an $80M Series D to complete its Phase 1 ER-100 trial in optic neuropathies and advance the Partial Epigenetic Reprogramming platform. Funding carries operations into H2 2027.

title: The Immune System Ages Differently in Men and Women
description: A Barcelona Supercomputing Center single-cell RNA sequencing study of ~1,000 people is the first to show sex-divergent immune aging - women trend toward inflammatory/autoimmune shifts while men show more pre-leukemic clones.

title: Rapamycin Might Blunt Exercise Response in Humans
description: New human data suggest chronic rapamycin may dampen the adaptive response to exercise, raising practical dosing questions for geroprotective use alongside resistance and Zone 2 training.

title: BioAge Reports Positive Phase 1 Data for BGE-102
description: BioAge Labs disclosed positive Phase 1 data for BGE-102, its NLRP3-inhibitor program for metabolic diseases of aging, supporting advancement to later-stage trials.

title: Engineered Stem Cells Become Lifelong Protein Factories
description: Researchers engineered stem cells that durably produce therapeutic proteins in vivo, a platform approach with potential for chronic age-related conditions.

title: Targeting an Appetite Hormone Receptor for Stronger Muscles
description: Preclinical work identifies a receptor-level lever to preserve muscle mass with age, complementing GLP-1-era concerns about sarcopenia during weight loss.

title: Vitamin C Alleviates Aging in Cynomolgus Monkeys
description: A primate study positions vitamin C as a translatable geroprotective lever by inhibiting ACSL4 and reducing ferroptosis-linked 'ferro-aging' pathways.

title: USPTO Publishes Senolytic Compound Patent Application
description: USPTO publication US20260097022A1 covers Bcl-2-binding senolytics identified via ML screens of 2,352 compounds and neural-net predictions over 800,000+ molecules.

title: WHOOP Raises $575M at $10.1B Valuation for AI Healthspan Platform
description: Series G led by Collaborative Fund with Mayo Clinic, Abbott, QIA and Mubadala values WHOOP at $10.1B on a $1.1B revenue run rate, with international expansion and an eventual IPO in view.

title: Life Biosciences Lands $80M to Push Cell Reset Therapy
description: Longevity.Technology's coverage of the Life Biosciences Series D frames the round as a watershed for epigenetic reprogramming, with Phase 1 ER-100 dosing now underway.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup March 2026
description: Monthly roundup covering resistance-training-driven brain aging reduction, heart aging review, NAD+ trial signals, and Cyclarity's 7-ketocholesterol paper, among other stories.

title: Finally! A Large Clinical Trial of Rapamycin for Healthy Aging
description: Dr. Matt Kaeberlein interviews Dr. Bonnie LaFleur of U. Arizona on the Coit-funded 700+ older adult RCT testing whether rapamycin improves resilience and immune aging.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches 84-Person Rapamycin Aging Trial
description: NIA-funded randomized placebo-controlled study in 84 older adults evaluates rapamycin dosing, safety, and both short- and long-term effects on healthy aging.

title: UT Health San Antonio to Test Rapamycin, Semaglutide, Dapagliflozin in 700+ Adults
description: One of the largest geroscience trials to date tests three existing drugs for anti-aging signals in over 700 older adults, led by the Barshop Institute.

title: Stem Cell Therapy Shows Promise for Reversing Aging-Related Frailty
description: Longeveron's Phase 2b of Lomecel-B reported dose-dependent 6MWT gains and 30.8% of patients moving to non-frail status in Cell Stem Cell.

title: Q1 2026 Longevity Biotech Funding Hits $3.74B Across 49 Rounds
description: DLT Chatbot records $3.74B raised Jan-Mar 2026 across 49 events, putting full-year 2026 on track for $8-9B and ~55-60% growth over 2025.

title: Mitrix Bio Moves Mitochondrial Transplants into the Clinic
description: Mitrix reports early Phase 1 safety in two older humans with no adverse events and has begun clinics under Right to Try for degenerative conditions.

title: David Sinclair Backs NAD Boosting Plus CD38 Inhibition as a Longevity Stack
description: Sinclair endorses a two-pronged NAD+ strategy - boost precursors while inhibiting the NAD-consuming enzyme CD38 - as a next-generation geroprotective stack.

title: Updated Longevity Protocols for 2026: Sinclair and Diamandis
description: Fresh protocol note from the Abundance Summit covering NMN 1g, resveratrol, nattokinase 10,000 units, spermidine, glycine, plant-based diet, pulsed fasting and 3-day fasts.

title: Bryan Johnson's Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update
description: Blueprint's 2026 refinements emphasize organ-specific metrics, frequent lab panels, reduced NMN/NR frequency, and adds low-dose lithium and NDGA.

title: Aging Guru Bryan Johnson's Supplement List for 2026
description: Johnson trims NMN/NR to six-days-weekly, confirms rapamycin removed, and adds low-dose lithium and NDGA to support brain and cellular function.

title: $67B Longevity Boom & Big Tech Health Partnerships - Week 16 Roundup
description: Weekly roundup: $67B market projection to 2035, Technogym x Google Cloud AI coaching, Function Health acquires GetLabs, Mitrix mitochondrial transplants, and clinical education marketplace launch.

title: Peloton Named Movement Partner for The Longevity Show 2026
description: Peloton's new Longevity Show partnership signals deeper consumer-fitness integration into the longevity ecosystem.

title: Generare Bags $21.6M for Nature-Derived Drug Leads
description: Biotech Generare raises $21.6M to advance natural-product derived drug leads relevant to longevity pathways.

title: Buck Institute Launches Healthspan Horizons
description: Healthspan Horizons is a new initiative to measure, understand, and extend healthspan, addressing one of the key gaps in modern longevity medicine.

title: Updated Estimates Challenge Bleak Picture of US State Gaps in Longevity
description: University of Wisconsin-Madison forecasts show universal cohort life expectancy gains across all US states and regions for those born 1941-2000.

title: Brown and Rochester Win Up to $22M to Test Censavudine for Aging
description: ARPA-H-funded project will test whether the HIV drug Censavudine can quiet DNA-triggered chronic inflammation in 200+ adults aged 60-65 over 48 weeks.

title: Rubedo Announces Positive Preliminary Results for RLS-1496 Senolytic
description: Rubedo's single-center ascending-dose RCT in plaque psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and skin aging reported preliminary positive results - one of the first senolytic clinical signals.
